reasoning: The stock experienced a significant ~15% jump following its Q1 2025 earnings report on April 22 (from $78.4 up to ~$89.5), driven by an EPS beat ($1.13 vs $1.01 expected). However, management chose to maintain, rather than raise, their full-year guidance, suggesting the rest of the year may be slightly weaker or management is being conservative due to tariff concerns. Because the full-year outlook wasn't upgraded, this earnings pop may be prone to a short-term fade or gap-fill as the initial momentum cools. I project a slight downside edge in the ultra-short term (1 week and 1 month) as profit-taking pressure prevails. Over the medium-to-long term (3 and 6 months), general equity market upward drift and the passage of time pull the probabilities back towards and slightly above 50%, reflecting an increasing level of uncertainty and the stock market's natural positive bias.
